findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 48. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Ensure the main landmark is at top level
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  The main landmark is contained in another landmark. [<main>]

findings.description: Ensure the document has at most one main landmark
findings.evidence:
Fix any of the following:
  Document has more than one main landmark [<main>]

Fix any of the following:
  The landmark must have a unique aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title to make landmarks distinguishable [<main>]

findings.description: Nested <main> elements: the HTML contains a <main> inside the <body>'s <main>, which is invalid HTML structure, though it does not affect content fidelity.
findings.evidence: The HTML has <main> at the top level wrapping everything, and then another <main> element after the <header> section.

findings.description: The table of contents items are rendered as internal anchor links (href='#history-background', etc.) which do not exist in the source PDF. These are navigational aids added by the converter, not fabricated external links, so this is a minor structural addition.
findings.evidence: HTML: History/Background etc. PDF has no hyperlinks in the table of contents.

findings.description: The header block information (State of North Carolina, address, officials' names/titles) appears both in the <header> section and is also present in the PDF as a single letterhead. This is a faithful reproduction of the letterhead, not problematic duplication.
findings.evidence: Header information appears once in the HTML header block, matching the PDF.
